subject: Physics
title: Gravity Is Not a Force
description: One-minute skill: Gravity Is Not a Force.

hook.text: Newton said gravity is a force pulling objects together. Einstein said Newton was wrong.

buildup.text: For 230 years, Newton's gravity worked perfectly. But it couldn't explain certain anomalies.

discovery.text: Einstein proposed gravity is geometry—mass warps spacetime, and objects follow the curves.

twist.text: Earth doesn't pull you down. The ground accelerates up into you through curved spacetime.

climax.text: This radical reframing explains everything Newton's gravity could, and more. It's more accurate.

punchline.text: Gravity isn't a force. It's the shape of spacetime.

quiz.question: How does Einstein describe gravity differently from Newton?
options: As the curvature of spacetime, not a force
As a stronger version of magnetism
As particles called gravitons pulling objects
